# Questions in Livestream 3

Live Stream #3: Thrice the Bugs, Thrice the Charm.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44</a> | What primary documents on ancient Egypt would you recommend for high school history students? | He recommends starting with a broad historical overview, such as Ian Shaw's works, because Egyptian history is too large to reduce to one definitive document. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=291" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:51</a> | How did you derive the 2312 date for Sargon? | He says the date comes from computer-based chronology modeling, which can process king lists and synchronisms more consistently than hand-built chronologies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:40</a> | What are your thoughts on the origins of Gnosticism? Is it a distinctly Egyptian phenomenon? | He describes Gnosticism as pre-Christian and as a synthesis of Egyptian religion and Neoplatonic thought that could re-allegorize other religions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=697" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:37</a> | Where in Ramesses II's reign do you think the exodus happened, and what evidence supports that view? | He places it around Ramesses II's 25th year, after the construction of Pi-Ramesses and near the death of the crown prince, with the Merneptah Stele setting an outer boundary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=943" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:43</a> | Is there historical evidence for a pharaoh having two queens at once? | Kings could have many wives, but only one Great Royal Wife at a time; succession and political marriages explain the larger royal harem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=1184" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:44</a> | Was Kadesh of the exodus near Petra/Wadi Musa or near Ein el-Qudeirat? | He is reserving judgment; the location of Kadesh-Barnea remains debated, and recent arguments have reopened the question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=1362" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:42</a> | Were the Shasu Israelites? If not, how widespread was worship of Yahweh? | No. He says the Shasu predate the exodus and were nomadic groups known mostly from Egyptian lists; the spread of Yahweh worship remains uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=1590" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:30</a> | Where do you think Mount Sinai is? | He does not claim a precise location, but thinks it was likely among mountains in southern Sinai; the evidence excludes some options more than it identifies one site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=1832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:32</a> | Did you see Holy Koolaid's video, and will you do a review? | He saw it but says he probably will not review it, because he views it as a distorted treatment rather than a serious work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=2057" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:17</a> | Is there evidence that Semites in Egypt worshiped golden calves? | He says not necessarily golden calves specifically, but there is broad evidence for bovine worship, and the golden calf episode fits a default Egyptian-style bovine worship pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=2255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:35</a> | Did the Israelite army of Moses have a forced retirement age like the Levites? | He thinks the army was more a practical muster of who could fight than a professional force with formal retirement rules. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=2376" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:36</a> | What is your favorite evidence for the exodus events? | He highlights the Merneptah Stele, the abandonment of Avaris, linguistic and toponymic evidence, and ritual-furniture parallels with the Ark of the Covenant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=2748" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:48</a> | How do secular scholars explain Egyptian loanwords in the Pentateuch, and why do they not see them as evidence for the exodus? | He says they often explain them through Egyptian influence in Canaan, but he thinks that requires too many assumptions and does not fit the broader linguistic pattern well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=3038" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:38</a> | Are there similarities between the tabernacle layout and Pharaoh's war-camp tent? | Yes. He says Ramesside reliefs show similar tent-within-a-tent patterns, but sees military tents as influenced by older religious tabernacle structures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=3246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:06</a> | How many scholars accept the existence of the Shasu, and if they accept the Shasu, why do they not accept the biblical exodus? | He says the Shasu and the people who became Israelites are separate issues; evidence for the Shasu is straightforward, while pre-Merneptah Israelite identity is harder to define archaeologically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=3500" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:20</a> | Do you take 1290 or 1279 as the beginning of Ramesses II's reign? | Neither. He prefers about 1288 BC, while noting that 1279 is still an acceptable date held by other scholars. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=3672" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:12</a> | Does the bird imagery in Jesus' lament over Jerusalem connect winged sun disks to Yahweh and Christ? | He sees a protective-wing motif behind the passage, related to Psalm 91 and winged imagery such as cherubim and Egyptian vulture symbolism, with some connection to winged sun disks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=4037" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:17</a> | Does Joshua have Egyptian toponyms and loanwords like the Pentateuch? | Yes, but less so; he says Egyptian vocabulary diminishes through Joshua, Judges, Kings, and the prophets as later linguistic influences increase. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=4220" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:20</a> | Do the Brooklyn Slave Papyrus or Ipuwer Papyrus provide a timeframe for the exodus? | No. The Brooklyn Papyrus has Semitic names used across a long period, and he dates Ipuwer's language to the 12th Dynasty, so neither narrows the exodus date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=4377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:57</a> | How could heavy Greek or Sea Peoples influence appear in the tribe of Dan? | He connects it to Dan's difficulty securing its inheritance and later settlement among regions with Neo-Hittite and Anatolian/Sea Peoples cultural influence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=4536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:36</a> | Do people dig specifically for manuscripts, and is there hope of finding more Christian papyri soon? | People generally do not dig only for manuscripts, but he says more Christian papyri may still appear, especially from the large unpublished Oxyrhynchus material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=4879" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:19</a> | What sources do you recommend on Nebuchadnezzar's conquest of Egypt? | He points to Mesopotamian and Babylonian annals rather than Egyptian sources, because Egyptian records generally avoid reporting defeats. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=5000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:20</a> | Was Egyptian *sDm.n=f* one verbal form, or did it hide two differently vocalized forms? | He accepts a second-tense use in line with Polotsky, but says the evidence does not let us reconstruct distinct pronunciations securely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=5253" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:33</a> | What are the origins of the ancient Egyptians? Were they originally from the Levant or Mesopotamia? | He says we do not know; migration from Mesopotamia is possible but unproven, and he favors caution because predynastic evidence is limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aauuHFdpbuU?t=5474" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:14</a> | What do you consider the blood in Exodus to be, regarding the Nile turning to blood? | He says ancient hearers would think of black, foul, undrinkable water rather than bright red liquid; the plague amplifies familiar Nile inundation phenomena into something abnormal. |
